message:
fix(engine): expand oracle release gate hash to cover all report fields

The previous content hash for OracleReleaseGateReport only hashed the
evaluations array, release_candidate_id, and epoch — omitting
schema_version, bead_id, policy_id, component, verdict, and all counter
fields. An attacker or buggy pipeline could swap the schema version,
policy ID, or verdict of a signed report without invalidating the hash,
undermining report integrity verification.

Changes:
- Extend `compute_report_hash()` signature to accept all twelve report
  fields (schema_version, bead_id, policy_id, component, evaluations,
  release_candidate_id, epoch, overall_verdict, pass/fail/advisory/
  inconclusive counts) and feed them into the SHA-256 digest
- Update `build_report()` and `verify_integrity()` call sites to pass
  all fields, ensuring any field mutation invalidates the hash
- Apply the same treatment to `compute_triage_hash()`: add
  schema_version, blocker/warning/info counts to the digest
- Update `build_triage_bundle()` and its `verify_integrity()` call sites
- Remove unused `sha2::Digest` import (now used via qualified path)
- Reorder imports to follow Rust convention (crate-local before external)
- Add enrichment and integration tests for hash-verified report building

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
